gpt-4o.notes: Functional step buttons and ray toggle are present, but the scene is not faithful to the textbook figure: wrong objects and colors, missing key labels, and the pinhole image-formation setup is misrepresented.

discrepancies: 3D objects (sphere, cone-tree, cube) do not match the 2D cartoon trees in the source
Tree trunk and foliage are too geometric and not composed of overlapping circles as in the original
Only one wall is shown, while the source has two converging walls
Pinhole barrier lacks prominent labeling and contextual shape as in the source
Rays colors (green, brown) and thicknesses don't match the source figure
gpt-4.1.notes: Interactivity is strong, but 3D primitives and color choices do not match the original, and the overall composition diverges significantly from the source.
action_items: Replace 3D cones/spheres/cubes with 2D-like overlapping circles to better echo the cartoon tree style.
Adjust wall geometry to resemble the two converging walls in the reference, rather than a single projection wall.
Refine ray drawing to use accurate colors and thicknesses as in the source (distinct green and brown rays).
Redesign the pinhole barrier to closely resemble the thick black divider with a clear round aperture.
Remove extraneous objects and hallucinated elements (e.g., the red cube and blue sphere) that do not appear in the original.

discrepancies: The generated scene contains three arbitrary shapes (cone tree, red box, blue sphere) instead of a single deciduous tree as in the original.
The projection on the wall uses three simple colored circular dots instead of an inverted, faded version of the tree.

The light rays in the original are specifically split into green (from leaves) and brown (from trunk), whereas the generation uses green, blue, and red rays corresponding to the three separate objects.
The black pinhole barrier in the original is a sharp, clean vertical rectangle with a highlighted 'Pinhole' label directly on the opening.
gemini-3.5-flash.notes: The simulation successfully demonstrates pinhole camera ray separation but fails visually by introducing unnecessary shapes (box, sphere) instead of representing the iconic inverted tree projection.
action_items: Replace the three separate primitive objects with a single, structured 3D deciduous tree matching the source illustration.
Render a faded, inverted and upside-down tree texture/shape on the wall during the pinhole state instead of generic circular dots.
Color-code the rays so they emanate from the foliage (green) and trunk (brown) specifically.
Refine the pinhole label and visual appearance to match the clean aesthetic of the original textbook diagram.
